reviews.review_title: Best potential to the wrong devs
reviews.review_description:
im leaving an honest review over here..

Just imagine putting your hard work into releasing a game to just never update it. That is basically this game but summarized.
It has been almost a full year without a proper update, and that leads to game breaking bugs discovered by the community.
There is a chance that if you click on any server, your headset will crash without any warning.
There is an invincibility glitch that's easy to do.
There is a very toxic community and is near impossible to find a normal server that lasts around 10 minutes without a game breaking bug.
If you want to have fun in this game, I'd say good luck.

Did I also mention that they had a Kickstarter that raised about $50k in usd.
So much money for such wasted potential that even people are asking for refunds.

This review wouldn't exist if there was consistent updates at least once or twice every 2 months

There is alot more to uncover but I can let you research that yourself.

Potential: 9.7/10
Updates: 0/10
Gameplay: 8.5/10
Community: -1/10
Replayability: 2/10

If you are looking to play this game and stumble across this video game, do not get this game unless this updates. No game deserved to be abandoned after release.

I think this game has a lot of potential, but it crashed my entire headset 3 times to the point where I had to restart it.

I think it could be way better if they added new maps and gamemodes and stuff and there aren't many people playing, in al of my time playing I think I have seen 2 full lobby's Max

But overal pretty fun game

reviews.review_title: Can’t wait to see what it will become…
reviews.review_description:
This game so far has been amazing, the community is ussally very nice  and/or funny. I have made a couple friends on it already. 
The overall gameplay is pretty good. I will admit that there is a lot of glitches and can be a good amount of lag if you don’t power off your headset before (or staying inside the game and plugging it in and leaving it to charge)plugging it in. The invincibility glitch is very annoying though, it does make it so you need to either get off or play another server. But other then the glitches and lag and the odd time yours and others mic audio doesn’t work… the game is great, I’m very exited to see what you will make of this game.

Hello Dev's
There has been recent problems in your almost dead game Tier one direct action, which is my most favorite VR game. There is a list of issues that need fixing please. I will list them down below.



-The Invincibility glitch is a really annoying glitch people use to get the upper hand in battles.
How to do it is by taking your non-suppressed pistol shooting your hand 4 times then taking a breaching charge putting it on the wall then picking back up and blowing up.


-Another one to bring back your game is to fix the crazy lag, I have a newest quest 3 model and 50% of the times it crashes before I can actually play, and sometimes my games outright just freezes.


-Another key thing to fix is the radio when you press tigger on head which should increase comms between inter team missions

Please take my suggestion to heart by applying these to your game. It took a lot of effort trying to get into contact with the true devs of this game so please try this out. A few lyal maybe 50 players play often and to bring this dead game back please do the listed, Thank you for listening!

I bought this game... I thought about refunding it but thought it would get some updates and get better. Now, months later there is still no single player campaign. There is nobody online to play with. And the tutorial level is just as janky as it was before. The worst part about it... Now it's a free game. I spent 15 bucks on something I could have gotten for free. It's that bad. You can't even give this game away and get players. Ugh. What a mess. The guns don't feel good, the graphics and sound are sophomoric at best. It's just terrible. I try to be constructive and polite in my reviews but this is a pile of crap and shouldn't even be allowed on the meta store.

I heard about the Kickstarter a while back to support this game, and I didn't really care. Then when the game was released, everybody said that it really sucked. I was just bored, and it is free to play, so I downloaded it on my quest. 20 minutes into playing and I couldn't really find anything I liked about the game. The controls are a bit unorthodox in my opinion, and the reloading and weapon handling is so unsatisfying. The maps weren't anything special, and the game also crashed multiple times within my small session of playing. There are also not that many players, so the lobbies were small for me. Overall, I just didn't find any of the gameplay or mechanics pleasing whatsoever. I hope they can fix this stuff soon, because that could get me engaged again.
The game has great potential. It’s just very laggy and servers are almost all dead if they would just fix the games lag and glitches, it would be one of my favorites

its good but its really new game and its laggy and its very hard to kill when your new but just stop giving it 1 star because its new like lol im a mod so i get why this is unfair (not in this game other games) this game does have a lot of potential so i think you should get it before i gets big!

This game is honestly a surprising hit. The gunplay feels really solid — I like how bullets actually come out of the barrel instead of unrealistically shooting from the scope like in a lot of other games. The recoil also feels great and satisfying.

That said, there are a few setbacks. Running feels clunky since you have to hold the joystick instead of just pressing it, and you can only sprint when pointing your gun down. The rope mechanics are also buggy — as soon as you touch them they freak out and jitter wildly, breaking immersion.

The online maps look great, but they’re way too big for only 5v5 matches. Climbing is another frustrating part: if your arm drifts too far from the ladder, you just fall, instead of automatically moving closer to grab on. On top of that, when you reach the top there’s a forced climbing animation. This feels unfair because if an enemy is waiting above, you’re stuck in the animation and can’t react. Falling is also far too fast and doesn’t feel realistic at all.

There are also small details that could make a big difference. For example, ropes should move when touched instead of your hand clipping through them. Grabbing doors looks awkward since your character grabs the handle upside-down. Breaching charges should make a sound when they’re live, and in the tutorial, if you die, you’re forced to restart the entire thing because there’s no respawn option.

Overall, the game is pretty good but could be great, but its free so I'm not complaining

So to summarize the game has its fair share of flaws as well as some good gameplay I would give it a 8.5/ 10
